LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF THOMAS TYRRELL'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED NOT TO PROCEED WITH ANY RENT BOARD PROCEEDING TO DETERMINE THE VALIDITY OF ANY COSTA-HAWKINS RENT INCREASE. THE COURT ACQUIRED JURISDICTION OVER THAT ISSUE BEFORE THE RENT BOARD, AND IT IS NOT APPROPRIATE FOR BOTH TRIBUNALS TO DECIDE THE SAME ISSUE SIMULTANEOUSLY. THIS ORDER DOES NOT AFFECT THE PARTIES' FREEDOM TO PURSUE OTHER CLAIMS, INCLUDING DECREASED SERVICES AT THE RENT BOARD. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: TRINA AYALA, CSR# 12964

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS PHILLIP FRANCISCO, AND SAMUEL MEHR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS GRANTED IN LIGHT OF DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEY'S AFFIDAVIT SHOWING THAT THE FAILURE TO FILE ON TIME WAS DUE TO HER MISTAKE OR NEGLECT. THE COURT ASKED THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER TO COME UP WITH A STIPULATION SETTING THE HEARING ON THE DEMURRER. ON THE SECOND CALL ON THE MATTER AT 9:45 A.M., THE PARTIES STIPULATED TO SET THE HEARING OF THE DEMURRER ON MAY 29, 2008. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION, 301, DEFENDANTS PHILLIP FRANCISCO AND SAMUEL MEHR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. SECTION 37.3(D)(2) OF THE RENT ORDINANCE EXCEPTS LANDLORDS PROCEEDING UNDER COSTA-HAWKINS FROM THE RENT ACT PROCEDURES, AND, AS INDICATED ON DEFENDANT'S EXHIBIT 2, SEEKING A RENT BOARD DETERMINATION IS OPTIONAL. THE DEFENDANT HAS 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF THOMAS TYRRELL'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction IS GRANTED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED NOT TO PROCEED WITH ANY RENT BOARD PROCEEDING TO DETERMINE THE VALIDITY OF ANY COSTA-HAWKINS RENT INCREASE. THE COURT ACQUIRED JURISDICTION OVER THAT ISSUE BEFORE THE RENT BOARD, AND IT IS NOT APPROPRIATE FOR BOTH TRIBUNALS TO DECIDE THE SAME ISSUE SIMULTANEOUSLY. THIS ORDER DOES NOT AFFECT THE PARTIES' FREEDOM TO PURSUE OTHER CLAIMS, INCLUDING DECREASED SERVICES AT THE RENT BOARD. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Trina Ayala CSR # 12964 (301/PJB/LB)

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS PHILLIP FRANCISCO AND SAMUEL MEHR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. SECTION 37.3(d)(2) OF THE RENT ORDINANCE EXCEPTS LANDLORDS PROCEEDING UNDER COSTA-HAWKINS FROM THE RENT ACT PROCEDURES, AND, AS INDICATED ON DEFENDANT'S EXHIBIT 2, SEEKING A RENT BOARD DETERMINATION IS OPTIONAL. THE DEFENDANT HAS 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =(301/PJB/AL)

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANTS PHILLIP FRANCISCO AND SAMUEL MEHR'S Motion To Set Aside Default IS GRANTED IN LIGHT OF DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEY'S AFFIDAVIT SHOWING THAT THE FAILURE TO FILE ON TIME WAS DUE TO HER MISTAKE OR NEGLECT. THE COURT ASKED THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER TO COME UP WITH A STIPULATION SETTING THE HEARING ON THE DEMURRER. ON THE SECOND CALL ON THE MATTER AT 9:45 A.M., THE PARTIES STIPULATED TO SET THE HEARING OF THE DEMURRER ON MAY 29, 2008. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =301/PJB/AL

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Apr-28-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: April 24, 2008

This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Steven R. Ruth, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Thomas Tyrrell, who was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants Phillip Francisco, Samuel Mehr, Jack Jackson or Lily Seaman.
Defaults entered against all defendants on March 26, 2008.
Doe defendants dismissed today, April 24, 2008.

Plaintiff introduces the following documentary evidence:

Exhibit 1---Handwritten note dated 01/09/08------------------------------------------ID/EVID.
Exhibit 2---Two page letter from plaintiffs Francisco & Mehr
to plaintiff Thomas Tyrrell dated November, 2007-------------------------ID/EVID.
Exhibit 3---Notice of Increase in Rent dated 01/24/08--------------------------------ID/EVID.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Thomas Tyrrell's request for declaratory relief as follows:

Defendants Phillip Francisco and Samuel Mehr no longer reside at the subject premises located at 1335 Stevenson Street, Upper Rear Unit, San Francisco, CA, and that rent on said premises has been increased to the fair market value of $2,200.00, effective April 1, 2008. Defendants Phillip Francisco, Samuel Mehr, Jack Jackson and Lily Seaman are responsible for payment of said rent increase. Additionally, plaintiff is awarded Costs in the amount of $652.40.

Counsel for plaintiff shall submit a proposed judgment to the Court for review not later than close of business on Thursday, April 24, 2008.
